title: Regular expressions with the global flag should be preferred with caution
description: Regular expressions in JavaScript can have a global flag (/g) that enables global searching and matching. While this flag can be useful in certain scenarios, it should be used with caution. When a regular expression has the global flag enabled, it remembers the position of the last match and continues searching for subsequent matches from that position.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Global flag (/g) in regex should be used with caution - it maintains state between calls
